DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; FOR TERMINATING, ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Douglas Holloway, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bank of the West.
Daniel Monberg, Vice President of Bank of the West, was placed under oath and examined.
Akop Baltayan, Esq. appeared for defendants Silver Hanger Cleaners, Inc. and Levon Davidian.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on November 6, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 22, 2010.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief was filed today, March 22, 2010.

Exhibit--1--Equipment Finance Agreement dated 6/14/07--------------------------------EVID/ID.
Exhibit--2--Personal Guaranty dated 6/14/07------------------------------------------EVID/ID.
Exhibit--3--Payoff calculation, undated----------------------------------------------EVID/ID.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bank of the Week, and against defendants Silver Hanger Cleaners, Inc. and Levon Davidian as follows:

Principal - $133,033.12.
Prejudgment Interest - $14,580.00.
Attorney Fees - $2,500.00.
Costs - $446.40.

Total - $150,559.52.

It is further Ordered that plaintiff, Bank of the West, is the owner of the leased equipment described on page 2 of the Judgment.

Counsel stipulates to the return of the Exhibits. Counsel for plaintiff agrees not to execute on this Judgment until Thursday, February 25, 2010.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Admissions Or In The Alternative, To Have Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted; For Sanctions; P/A; Separate Statement; Declaration Of Douglas Holloway In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Douglas Holloway, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Bank of the West
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion For Order Compelling Further Answers To Interrogatories And Request For Admissions, or in the alternative, To Have Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted is granted as it seeks an order deeming Plaintiff's Requests For Admissions admitted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond further was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to further respond to Plaintiff's form interrogatories within 10 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $415, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Complelling Aswers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; For Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Douglas Holloway, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Bank of the West
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion For Order Compelling Further Answers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; For Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary, And Monetary Sanctions is granted as it seeks an order compelling further answers to special interrogatories and Request For Production Of Documents. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 10 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $490, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And That Requests For Admissins Be Deemed Admitted; For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities ; Declaration
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Douglas Holloway, Esq. (415-285-6226), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Bank of the West
Akop Baltayan, Esq. (626-372-0399), appearing for defendant / opposing party Silver Hanger Cleaners, Inc.

RULING:
Plaintiff's Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And For Order Deeming Request For Admissions Admitted. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED as modified. Motion is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's interrogatories within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $265, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
